**Architecture And Separation Of Concerns**

No blocking boundary violation found. The changed Rust code is limited to validation/gate preset comments and tests. The container resolver remains pure over an in-memory issue slice in [crates/jit/src/validation/graph.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/validation/graph.rs:1003), with no storage or CLI coupling.

**Functional Paradigm**

No blocking issue found. The resolver uses iterator-based matching and keeps side effects out of domain validation logic at [crates/jit/src/validation/graph.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/validation/graph.rs:1010).

**Correctness And Invariants**

No blocking invariant issue found. Event logging covers the created issues, gate additions, and state changes in [.jit/events.jsonl](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/.jit/events.jsonl:1).

**Safety**

No unsafe code was introduced in the reviewed changes. No PID-sensitive code was touched.

No new fallible production path or new library `unwrap()`/`expect()` issue was introduced in the reviewed changes.

See [docs/concepts/planning-bracket.md](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/docs/concepts/planning-bracket.md:54) and [docs/concepts/planning-bracket.md](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/docs/concepts/planning-bracket.md:68).
